  • Constructor Details

    • ResolveMemberReference

      public ResolveMemberReference(NameReference reference, CrossReferenceServiceConfiguration sc)

  • Method Details

    • analyze

      public ProblemReport analyze()
      Analys a MemberReference. Considering the following example: 
       class B ...
 class G<E> { E m() {...} }

 ...
 G<B> g = new G<B>();
 B b = g.m();
 ...
      The reference g.m() is the one under test. Several types will show up:
      • declarationType - the type of the member at its declaration. Here the type of G.m() which is E.
      • genericFreeDeclaraionType - the type of the declaration in a non-generic situation, which is java.lang.Object here.
      • kernelType - if declarationType is an array, kernelType will be the component type (with all [] removed)
      • actualType - the type of the member in the parameterized instance: here the type of G<B>.m() which is B.
      • genericFreeType - if the actualType is a TV itsself, this is the type that it will be replaced in a non-generic situation (Object or first boundary).
      • resolvedType - if there are multiple bounds the reference might have to be cast to a different one than the first.
      Also, if there are explicit type parameters, they will be removed.
      Overrides:
      analyze in class TwoPassTransformation
      Returns:
      a problem report, an instance of NoProblemif all requirements have been met.

    • transform

      public void transform()
      Description copied from class: TwoPassTransformation
      Performs the syntactic transformation. This method may not call any query that may lead to a model update. If this transformation is visible, change reports are send to the change history. The default implementation will check if the problem report has been initialized, and report the begin of the transformation to the change history if this transformation is visible. Redefined methods should call this method via super.transform() as the first action.
      Overrides:
      transform in class TwoPassTransformation
      See Also:
      • TwoPassTransformation.analyze()
      • Transformation.isVisible()
      • ChangeHistory.begin(Transformation)
